objective: Fix execution-scope validation so reusable governance PR checkouts can validate trusted scopes while detached by using GitHub PR branch context.
scope_boundary: Issue #324 planning, execution scope, progress, fail-fast, and validation evidence
out_of_scope: Changing GitHub checkout behavior in the reusable workflow
research_summary: EmailAssistant PR #3 proved the reusable workflow checks out pull request merge refs in detached HEAD state. The current validator only reads git branch --show-current, so it rejects a valid trusted scope before path validation.

sprints.name: Planning scope
sprints.goal: Land a trusted execution scope before validator source edits.
acceptance_criteria: Execution scope covers only issue #324 implementation files
Planning PR contains no validator source edits

sprints.name: Validator implementation
sprints.goal: Allow detached PR checkouts to use GitHub branch context while preserving local branch enforcement.
tasks: Rerun downstream EmailAssistant PR #3 governance gate
acceptance_criteria: Detached checkout with GITHUB_HEAD_REF passes when it matches expected_branch
EmailAssistant PR #3 governance check passes after merge

specialist_reviews.focus: EmailAssistant PR #3 reusable workflow failure mode
specialist_reviews.summary: The failure is a detached-checkout branch detection issue in the shared validator.

alternate_model_review.summary: No alternate model connector is available in this session; same-family execution is constrained by scope and regression tests.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Proceed with the detached-checkout validator fix after this planning scope lands on base.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Patch branch detection and tests, then rerun EmailAssistant PR #3 governance check.

material_deviation_rules: Do not remove branch validation for normal local checkouts.
If workflow behavior is changed instead of validator behavior, update the plan before implementation.
